issues: panel occupies ~38% of screen width and ~37% of screen height, dominating the game world view and reducing toy-diorama feel
dialog panel is significantly larger than the 32x32 player character visible at left edge, breaking relative scale hierarchy
two-section panel layout creates a wallpaper-like fullscreen overlay impression rather than a small in-world popup
panel decorative corner icons (~30x30px) are at the upper limit for HUD icon budget
reasoning: The panel_dialog_v01 composite shows a large dialog panel centered on screen measuring approximately 480x260px against a 1280x690 viewport. As a UI overlay panel this is a different category than world entities, so direct player-size comparison is less critical, but the panel still occupies a substantial portion of the playfield. The top golden section with decorative scroll corners and the bottom cream text area together create a visually heavy overlay. The panel width (~38% of screen) and its vertical positioning over the center of the play area significantly obscures the game world, pushing toward a wallpaper/modal feel rather than a lightweight diorama hint. Corner ornament icons appear around 28-32px which edges past the ≤24px HUD icon guideline. The pixel-art style is consistent with the scene aesthetic and perspective is flat/top-down matching the game. Score 6: style matches but size is marginal-to-large for a diorama feel.
